name: Em Sherif
description: About Em Sherif is a renowned upscale restaurant in Beirut, known for its authentic Lebanese cuisine and fine-dining experience. Founded by self-taught chef Mireille Hayek in 2010, the restaurant aims to recreate the warmth and hospitality of traditional Lebanese households through its home-cooked meals. The ambiance is designed to resemble a luxurious Lebanese home, with antique-style decor creating a nostalgic atmosphere.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Indulge in an extraordinary culinary experience featuring authentic Lebanese cuisine with rich flavors and delicate presentations 2 Enjoy live music entertainment while savoring traditional Lebanese dishes like Kibbeh with yogurt and decadent desserts 3 Experience the luxurious ambiance resembling an antique oriental mansion for a classy dining atmosphere
reviews_summary: Em Sherif is a highly acclaimed Lebanese fine-dining restaurant in Beirut, known for its exquisite set menu offering a plethora of delicious and delicate dishes. Reviewers praise the scrumptious yet refined flavors of dishes like minced meat Rkakat, Sambousek, Kibbeh with yogurt, and a unique muffin-shaped puff pastry filled with rice and condiments. The ambiance is described as elegant and nostalgic, enhanced by live music performances. However, some reviewers caution about the large portions served in the set menu that may lead to overeating.
tips: Consider pacing yourself during the meal due to the generous portions served in the set menu | Visit on weekends to enjoy live music performances reflecting old Lebanese songs for added cultural immersion | Avoid sitting near areas where smoking (cigars, shisha) is allowed if you are sensitive to smoke

name: Liza
description: About Liza Soughayar, known for her eponymous Paris restaurant serving Lebanese cuisine, took a bold step by opening Liza Beirut in her home city. Housed in a renovated 19th-century mansion adorned with challenging artworks by young Lebanese artists, the restaurant features dining rooms with unique decor such as wallpaper replicating old Lebanese Pound banknotes and silhouettes of bombed Beirut buildings.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience comforting and fancy ambiance 2 Enjoy delicious food with great presentation 3 Explore a national treasure with beautiful architecture
reviews_summary: Liza is a renowned restaurant in Beirut known for its comforting and fancy ambiance, delicious food, and beautiful architecture. Reviewers praise the dessert selection, friendly staff, quick service, professional presentation of dishes, and the overall classy atmosphere. However, some mention small portions and high prices as drawbacks. The restaurant is highlighted for being a national treasure with stunning inside and outside architecture that makes it perfect for personal celebratory dinners.
tips: Try their desserts if you have a sweet tooth | Consider valet parking due to limited parking spaces in the area | Avoid peak times or special events like weddings to ensure a more peaceful dining experience

name: Mayrig
address_full: 282 Pasteur Street, Mansour Building, Beirut, Lebanon
description: About Mayrig is a renowned Armenian restaurant in Beirut, known for its traditional dishes made with fresh ingredients and silk road spices. The restaurant has gained popularity over the years, attracting both locals and expats. Despite being affected by the Beirut Blast, Mayrig was able to make a comeback with the help of crowd-funding. Its success has led to the opening of another branch in Cairo, Egypt.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Savor delicious Armenian cuisine with a unique twist on Lebanese favorites 2 Enjoy generous portions of flavorful dishes like kabab kerez and manti 3 Experience warm ambiance and attentive service in a cozy setting
reviews_summary: Mayrig offers a delightful dining experience with flavorful Armenian dishes and generous portions. Reviewers praise the delicious kabab kerez, manti, and Lebanese favorites like hummus and fattoush with an Armenian twist. The warm ambiance, attentive service, and friendly staff create a cozy atmosphere for enjoying homemade comfort food. While some mention that certain plates could be improved, overall Mayrig is highly recommended for its authentic flavors and welcoming hospitality.
tips: Try the kabab kerez dish with a side of yogurt for an extra tasty experience | Ask the staff for food recommendations to discover outstanding dishes | Visit during off-peak hours to avoid long wait times due to busyness

name: Babel Bay
description: About Babel Bay is a chic and popular restaurant located in Beirut, Lebanon, known for its flavorful seafood dishes made with fresh ingredients. The interior is designed to be homey and attentive, featuring a small library, a wooden bar, and a study area. It's an ideal spot for couples looking for a romantic dining experience with delectable food in a private setting. The restaurant offers unique flavors and exceptional preparations that take seafood to the next level.
menu_items: Gambas al ajillo,Shrimp,Fried shrimp,Batata harra,Baguette,Shrimp and grits,Seafood,Fried fish,Hummus,Shaved ice
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Indulge in high-quality seafood dishes with Lebanese twists 2 Experience impeccable service and warm hospitality 3 Enjoy complimentary desserts and fruits at the end of your meal
reviews_summary: Babel Bay is a high-end restaurant in Beirut's Zaytouna Bay known for its impeccable service, delicious seafood dishes, and complimentary desserts. Reviewers praise the friendly staff, cozy yet stylish atmosphere, and beautifully presented plates bursting with flavor. While some mention minor disappointments with certain dishes like vine leaves or raw fish platter, overall recommendations highlight the exceptional quality of food like shrimps fattet and white fish raw. The restaurant's attention to detail in presentation and service make it a top choice for tourists seeking a luxurious dining experience.
tips: Try signature dishes like shrimps fattet or white fish raw for an exceptional culinary experience | Request seating away from heavily trafficked areas to enhance your dining ambiance | Visit during weekdays for a quieter dining experience compared to weekends | Make reservations in advance as Babel Bay can get busy especially during peak hours

name: Tawlet
description: About Nestled in the vibrant Armenian quarter of Bourj Hammoud, Tawlet is a culinary gem that embodies the spirit of Lebanese cuisine. Founded by Kamal Mouzawak as part of the Souk el Tayeb initiative, this restaurant connects city dwellers with local farmers, showcasing fresh produce in a delightful buffet format. Each day at Tawlet features a guest chef from various villages across Lebanon, offering diners an authentic taste of regional specialties.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience diverse regional variations of Lebanese cuisine prepared by guest chefs from different villages daily 2 Enjoy fresh ingredients sourced directly from small-scale farmers at Souk el Tayeb market 3 Partake in cooking classes to delve deeper into Lebanon's culinary culture
reviews_summary: Tawlet in Beirut is a standout restaurant praised for its delicious and healthy Lebanese cuisine. Reviewers rave about the exceptional talent of chef Laure Ghosn, highlighting dishes like batata mechwiyé, warak enab b zeit, and sambousik bé loz w Joz. The freshness and flawless execution of every item impress diners, with mentions of outstanding salads, roast beef in pomegranate sauce, and traditional Lebanese desserts. Guests appreciate the cozy atmosphere, varied buffet lunch with modern twists on traditional dishes, and attentive service from the executive chef.
tips: Visit during lunchtime to enjoy the all-you-can-eat buffet featuring a variety of traditional Lebanese dishes with modern twists | Try the lemonade with rose water as a refreshing addition to your meal | Engage with the chefs who are happy to explain each dish's background and offer insights into local cuisine | Consider joining cooking classes offered at Tawlet to learn more about Lebanese culinary traditions

name: Le Sushi Bar
address_full: New Starco Building, Rizkallah Street Minet El-Hosn Sector, Beirut, Lebanon
description: About Le Sushi Bar, established in 1998 by Mario Haddad, is a pioneer of sushi in the Middle East. It introduced popular dishes like crazy maki and volcano maki, now widely replicated. The restaurant won the Restaurant & Bar Design Awards in 2020 for its stunning interiors by Gregory Gatserelia. Despite being pricier than other options, patrons believe that the excellence of Japanese cuisine justifies the cost.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience exceptional sushi dishes with high-quality ingredients 2 Enjoy an intimate and elegant ambiance ideal for special occasions or date nights 3 Receive top-notch service from friendly and knowledgeable staff members
reviews_summary: Le Sushi Bar in Beirut is a must-visit for sushi lovers seeking an exceptional dining experience. The ambiance is intimate and elegant, perfect for a date night. The staff are friendly, attentive, and knowledgeable, ensuring impeccable service. Dishes like marinated salmon, seared scallops, crab salad, and black cod are highly recommended for their exquisite taste and presentation. While the prices are on the higher side, the quality of food and service justifies the cost. Overall, Le Sushi Bar offers outstanding sushi in a refined setting that guarantees a memorable dining experience.
tips: For a more affordable visit without compromising quality or experience, consider sharing dishes with your group to try multiple items. | If you prefer outdoor seating with a casual vibe while enjoying traditional Japanese offerings along with signature dishes at Le Sushi Bar's new location in Downtown Beirut. | To avoid disappointment with certain menu items like crispy squid mentioned as chewy by some reviewers; opt for highly recommended options such as marinated salmon or black cod. | Visit during off-peak hours to enjoy quicker service; consider making reservations ahead of time especially during weekends or peak dining hours.

name: Barbar-Hamra
address_full: VFWH+68C، PIccadilly Street, Hamra, Beirut, Lebanon
description: About Barbar-Hamra is a beloved spot in Beirut, celebrated for its comforting and straightforward Lebanese fast food that won’t break the bank. This eatery offers an impressive selection of grilled meats, fried delights, and vegetarian options alongside tempting desserts. The crunchy falafels and stuffed shawarmas have garnered a loyal following among locals and visitors alike. Many rave about the tender beef shawarma, which may be small but packs a flavorful punch with its decadent tahini sauce.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience authentic Lebanese street food at an iconic 24/7 restaurant 2 Enjoy flavorful dishes such as chicken shawarma sandwiches and grape leaves 3 Try the best falafel in town at affordable prices
reviews_summary: Barbar-Hamra in Beirut, Lebanon, is a popular 24/7 restaurant known for its great reputation among locals for taste and volume. The atmosphere is described as uplifting and cosy, with friendly service from young staff. While the prices are reasonable considering Lebanon's high price standard, the flavor of dishes like the chicken shawarma sandwich is highlighted. Some reviewers mention fast service, affordable prices, and delicious food options like falafel and shawarma sandwiches.
tips: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds and noise | Expect fast service but be prepared for limited seating options | Consider takeout if you prefer a quieter dining experience or want to enjoy your meal elsewhere

name: M. Sahyoun Falafel
description: About M. Sahyoun Falafel is a renowned establishment known for serving the ultimate falafel sandwiches, with a secret recipe that dates back to 1935. The shop is part of a pair of adjacent establishments owned by two brothers who are no longer on speaking terms, each offering similar menus and sparking debates about which one is superior. Visitors can expect tasty falafel, reasonable prices, and a welcoming atmosphere at this classic hole-in-the-wall spot in Beirut.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience authentic and flavorful falafel sandwiches at reasonable prices 2 Witness the hands-on approach of the dedicated owner wrapping sandwiches with precision 3 Enjoy generous portions served alongside fresh pita bread, vibrant salads, and creamy tahini sauce
reviews_summary: M. Sahyoun Falafel is a must-visit spot in Beirut, Lebanon, known for its authentic and flavorful falafel sandwiches at reasonable prices. The owner's hands-on approach and dedication to quality make this place stand out among the rest. Visitors rave about the crispy yet soft falafel, fresh pita bread, vibrant salads, and creamy tahini sauce that create a burst of traditional flavors with each bite. The generous portions and budget-friendly prices cater to both long-time falafel enthusiasts and first-time visitors alike. With a rich history dating back to 1935, M. Sahyoun continues to impress locals and tourists with its delicious offerings.
tips: Visit during off-peak hours or be prepared for potential crowds as it is a popular spot | Opt for the big sandwich option if you want a more satisfying meal | Observe the falafel frying process and sandwich making for an immersive experience | Ensure you are eating from the original shop by looking out for signs of satisfaction like other customers' happiness

name: Ferdinand
description: About Ferdinand is a European pub located in Beirut, Lebanon. It has received 369 reviews and offers a menu with a range of prices. The restaurant is listed as one of the best spots for French brasseries and bistros in Lebanon. It's known for its relaxed and trendy environment, serving delicious dishes at affordable prices. Additionally, it's praised for its great service and laid-back atmosphere, making it a popular choice among visitors to Lebanon.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Enjoy delicious food such as the highly acclaimed Ferdinand Burger and chicken quesadillas 2 Experience unique cocktails with special touches recommended by knowledgeable staff 3 Chill in the great outdoor area while sipping on yum drinks
reviews_summary: Ferdinand is a trendy and welcoming cocktail bar in Beirut, known for its cool atmosphere, friendly service, tasty food like the Ferdinand Burger and chicken quesadillas, unique cocktails with special touches recommended by knowledgeable staff like Walid, and a great outdoor area to chill. The place offers a variety of local and small batch alcohols to try, making it a must-visit spot for those looking for quality drinks and pub food.
tips: Ask the waiter for recommendations on best-selling dishes and cocktails for an enhanced experience | Visit during happy hour to enjoy discounted drinks | Book your table in advance especially on weekends to secure seating at this popular spot | If you're into gin-based fruity drinks, try Walid's recommendation of Thai Treat followed by Peppercorn Cup

name: Onno Bistro
description: About Onno Bistro is a Lebanese-Armenian fusion restaurant that offers a rich culinary experience. The menu features dishes like cracked bulgur wheat with chili, kibbeh of raw lamb and lentils, boreg filled with sheep's milk cheese, hummus, stuffed grape leaves, and batata harra. With generous portion sizes and reasonable prices, this restaurant has become a culinary landmark in Bourj Hammoud.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Savor authentic Lebanese-Armenian fusion cuisine bursting with flavors like Bulgur Salad (Itch) and ground beef Manti. 2 Enjoy top-notch service in a cozy atmosphere that adds to the culinary adventure. 3 Indulge in delicious desserts served generously after your meal.
reviews_summary: Onno Bistro is a hidden gem offering a fusion of Lebanese and Armenian cuisine, where guests rave about the delicious Bulgur Salad (Itch), ground beef Manti, kofte yogurt soup (Madzounov), lahmajun, and chocolate pistachio cake. Despite limited parking and close quarters seating with smoking allowed, the restaurant's friendly service and top-notch dessert selection make it worth the visit. The menu bursts with mouthwatering dishes prepared with fresh ingredients in a lean and delicious way, making Onno Bistro a go-to spot for an unforgettable dining experience.
tips: Make reservations in advance due to limited seating capacity. | Consider visiting during off-peak hours to avoid crowds. | Try unique dishes like kofte yogurt soup (Madzounov) for an authentic taste experience.

name: T-Marbouta
address_full: HAMRA SQUARE, GROUND FLOOR, Beirut, Lebanon
description: About T-Marbouta, located in the Hamra quarter, is a charming restaurant offering a relaxed and colorful environment where guests can savor a variety of authentic Lebanese dishes. The menu features an array of starters and mains, including traditional Lebanese dips, meats, and vegetarian options. With its extensive vegan menu, the restaurant caters to diverse dietary preferences. Local patrons frequent this hidden gem for its mouthwatering Lebanese cuisine and inviting ambiance.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience authentic Lebanese cuisine in a cozy setting with old Beirut vibes 2 Enjoy a variety of delicious mezze dishes such as hummus, kebabs, lentil soup, felafel, and more 3 Partake in cultural events like film screenings and live music while savoring traditional Lebanese food
reviews_summary: T-Marbouta is a beloved restaurant in Hamra, Beirut, known for its delicious Lebanese mezze and cozy atmosphere. Reviewers praise the friendly staff, tasty food offerings like kebabs and salads, and the authentic old Beirut vibes. Some mention minor issues like dry kafta aarayis or smoke from shisha but overall recommend this spot for a satisfying dining experience.
tips: For non-smokers, request seating indoors to avoid exposure to cigarette smoke | Try their specialties like labne with garlic, chicken msakhan, cheese rolls, tawook dish for an authentic taste of Lebanon | If visiting during peak hours when the restaurant is busy, be patient with service as waiters may be occupied

name: BARON
description: About Baron, located in Beirut, is a European pub that offers a relaxed and trendy environment with beautifully plated dishes at an affordable price. Founded in 2016 by chef Athanasios Kargatzidis, also known as Tommy, the restaurant focuses on showcasing local produce and features an ever-changing fine-dining menu based on market availability and fresh catches.
menu_items: Green curry,Ice cream,Duck confit,Ramen,Quinoa,Brussels Sprouts,Banana split,Sesame,Bagel,Steak
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience a chic and cozy ambiance ideal for a date night or special occasion 2 Try exceptional dishes like the Soujouk Dates, Calamari, and Feta that stand out for their flavors 3 Explore an original menu that offers unique twists on traditional Lebanese cuisine
reviews_summary: Baron is a chic and cozy restaurant with a great ambiance, perfect for a date night or fancy gathering. While some dishes like the Soujouk Dates, Calamari, and Feta were exceptional, others fell short in terms of taste and portion size. The service is friendly and attentive, but the high prices may not always be justified by the overall dining experience. Despite mixed reviews on certain dishes, Baron stands out for its original menu showcasing Lebanese flavors in unique ways.
tips: Consider sharing plates as portions can vary in size | Don't miss trying the recommended dishes like Soujouk Dates and Feta | Be prepared for higher prices compared to portion sizes offered | Visit during peak hours to enjoy the lively atmosphere

name: Jai asian Restaurant
description: About Jai Asian Restaurant in Beirut offers a relaxed and stylish setting with contemporary furniture. The menu features a variety of spicy Indian, Thai, and Vietnamese dishes such as chicken, shrimp rolls, fish cakes, and noodle soups seasoned with coriander, cumin, ginger, or curry. The open kitchen creates a tranquil atmosphere where patrons can enjoy high-quality food prepared by Chef Wael Lazkani.
menu_items: Steamed dumplings,Kombucha,Chicken curry,Beef,Pakora,Larb,Green curry,Sesame chicken,Spring roll,Soup,Curry,Noodle,Bulgogi
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience Arab chefs preparing Indian and Thai cuisine for a unique twist on familiar dishes 2 Enjoy delicious food with standout items like pad Thai noodles and butter chicken 3 Savor an authentic holiday-like ambiance at this small yet charming restaurant
reviews_summary: Jai Asian Restaurant in Beirut offers a unique dining experience with its cozy and intimate setting, where seating is limited but the food quality shines. The chefs, predominantly Arabs, bring a distinct touch to the Indian and Thai cuisine served. While some dishes like the PHO soup may miss the mark, others like the pad Thai noodles and butter chicken impress diners. The ambiance exudes holiday vibes, complemented by friendly staff and affordable prices. Complimentary desserts add a sweet touch to an already delightful meal.
tips: Be patient for a table as seating is limited; consider making reservations or visiting during off-peak hours | Try their standout dishes like pad Thai noodles and butter chicken for a flavorful experience | Ask for their flavorful chili sauce if you enjoy spicy condiments with your meal | If waiting outside on the curb is uncomfortable, call ahead to check availability or plan your visit around good weather conditions

name: Society
description: About Society Bistro, located in Saifi Suites, is a popular dining spot known for its exquisite Italian and French cuisine. Despite being in business for a long time, it has gained recent attention for offering high-quality food at reasonable prices. The restaurant takes pride in serving continental breakfast and business lunch while maintaining a chic and welcoming ambiance. With positive feedback contributing to its popularity, Society Bistro is often fully booked due to its beautiful interior and professional service.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Enjoy a diverse culinary adventure with exquisite Italian and French cuisine 2 Experience well-crafted dishes using quality ingredients at reasonable prices 3 Relish in a cozy yet refined atmosphere with friendly and attentive service
reviews_summary: Society Bistro in Beirut offers an outstanding dining experience with a delicious variety of Italian and French dishes made from high-quality ingredients at reasonable prices. Reviewers praise the flavorful food, cozy atmosphere, and attentive service. While some mention minor improvements needed in certain dishes or service quality, overall, Society Bistro stands out for its value and memorable dining experiences.
tips: Make reservations ahead of time as the restaurant tends to be fully booked | Try the Caesar salad, bresaola pizza, poulet mariné for standout flavors | Consider ordering tagliatelle mushroom for a rich and creamy dish | Be aware that while some reviewers mention minor flaws in service or food quality, the overall value makes it highly recommended

name: Al Falamanki Raouche
description: About Al Falamanki Raouche offers a delightful Lebanese brunch experience every Saturday, featuring a buffet of traditional Lebanese dishes such as hummus, fattoush, foul, kebbe and manaouche. The restaurant's traditional decor and beautiful ceramic tiles create an atmosphere that celebrates Lebanese culture. Guests can enjoy shisha and play backgammon while savoring Arabic delicacies.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Enjoy delicious Lebanese cuisine made from fresh ingredients 2 Experience breathtaking sea views while dining 3 Family-friendly atmosphere suitable for all diners
reviews_summary: Al Falamanki Raouche offers a delightful dining experience with stunning sea views overlooking the iconic Raouche Rock. The food is described as delicious, prepared with fresh and high-quality ingredients, showcasing traditional Lebanese cuisine. Guests particularly enjoyed dishes like tabbouleh, ice cream with kashta, labneh, foul, manakish, and more. The ambiance is praised for being family-friendly and welcoming for all diners. While some found the prices to be on the higher side, the excellent service provided by friendly staff members like Jad enhanced the overall experience.
tips: Try popular dishes like tabbouleh, ice cream with kashta, labneh, foul, manakish | Request a glass instead of plastic cups for water if presentation matters to you | Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds and enjoy a more relaxed dining experience | Consider budgeting accordingly as prices are on the higher side

name: Cocteau Restaurant
description: About Cocteau Restaurant is a world-renowned French restaurant located in the heart of Beirut. The restaurant offers a wide range of dishes, including steaks and salads. The service is excellent and the atmosphere elegant. The prices are on the higher side, but it is worth it for the quality of food.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Indulge in superb French cuisine including fresh crab salad and exquisite bouillabaisse 2 Experience exceptional hospitality from Pierre Freije for a truly unforgettable dining experience 3 Enjoy a romantic evening or business entertainment in the chic retro interiors of Cocteau
reviews_summary: Cocteau Restaurant in Beirut is highly praised for its exceptional food quality and service. Reviewers highlight the delicious salmon tartar, perfectly grilled steak, and rich pain perdu. The ambiance is charming with excellent decor, while Pierre Freije's hospitality stands out. Despite being on the pricey side, Cocteau offers a delightful dining experience with top-notch cleanliness, taste of food, menu variety, service, and atmosphere.
tips: Make reservations in advance to secure a table at this popular upscale restaurant | Visit during winter for hearty dishes like bouillabaisse that are well-suited for the season | Consider trying classic dishes as well as innovative items inspired by local ingredients on their ever-changing menu

name: Appetito Trattoria Gemmayzeh
description: About Nestled in the heart of Beirut, Appetito Trattoria Gemmayzeh offers an authentic Italian dining experience. The trattoria boasts a menu filled with mouth-watering antipasti, pizzas, pastas, and experimental dishes that blend classic Mediterranean flavors. The rustic interior exudes coziness and is adorned with paintings of the Tuscan countryside.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience authentic Italian cuisine in a cozy setting 2 Try the impressive Gnocchi Alla Siciliana and delicious pizza 3 Enjoy warm hospitality from friendly and attentive staff
reviews_summary: Appetito Trattoria Gemmayzeh is a cozy Italian restaurant with a warm and inviting atmosphere. Reviewers praise the delicious food, particularly highlighting the Gnocchi Alla Siciliana and pizza. The staff is friendly and attentive, although some mention slight service delays. The decor creates an authentic Italian feel, perfect for romantic dates or small gatherings. While there were minor issues like missing utensils in one review, overall, guests highly recommend this place for its tasty dishes and top-notch service.
tips: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid potential service delays when the restaurant is fully booked | Don't miss trying the Gnocchi Alla Siciliana and thin-crust pizzas which are highly recommended by reviewers | Engage with the staff who are described as polite and accommodating to enhance your dining experience | Take part in unique experiences like spinning the wheel game at the entrance for a chance to win free items from the menu

name: BUCO
description: About BUCO in Batroun offers a luxurious and laid-back dining experience with stunning views of the coast. The restaurant is known for its rustic elegance and inviting ambiance. While it's popular for its burgers, the menu also features a variety of appetizers perfect for sharing. Reservations are recommended due to its popularity. The restaurant in Saifi on Gouraud street is often busy, so booking ahead is advisable.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience trendy dining in a beautiful setting in Beirut 2 Try the standout Mulberry Bacon burger with unique flavor combinations 3 Enjoy juicy and flavorful steaks at Buco
reviews_summary: Buco in Beirut offers a trendy and stylish dining space with solid food options, particularly highlighted by their juicy and flavorful steaks. The burgers are popular but opinions vary on them. The restaurant's ambience is praised for being perfect for a relaxed meal in the heart of the city. One standout burger is the Mulberry Bacon, known for its fresh and juicy Australian grain-fed meat, crispy onion crunch, and unique mulberry jam that balances sweet-salty flavors perfectly. The staff receives high praise for their service quality.
tips: Make reservations to ensure seating preference as there have been issues reported with outdoor table bookings | For burger enthusiasts, don't miss trying the Mulberry Bacon burger known for its balanced sweet-salty flavors from mulberry jam | Visit during off-peak hours to avoid noise and crowds indoors if looking for a quieter experience | Engage with friendly staff like Hadi who enhance the overall dining experience

name: Al Falamanki Sodeco
description: About Al Falamanki Sodeco is a relaxed and intimate restaurant located in the heart of Beirut. It offers a traditional style and laid-back vibe, making it the perfect place for a midday meal after exploring Downtown Beirut. The restaurant is known for its fine dining experience and sophisticated Levantine dishes, providing an ideal setting for dining under the stars.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Enjoy Lebanese dishes in a charming setting that captures the essence of old Beirut 2 Picturesque outdoor seating surrounded by lush greenery 3 Rich selection of flavorful Lebanese favorites including kebabs and mezze
reviews_summary: Al Falamanki Sodeco in Beirut is a gem of a restaurant that exudes the charm and spirit of old Beirut. The setting is picturesque, perfect for both day and night dining, where patrons can enjoy beautifully arranged outdoor seating surrounded by lush greenery. The menu offers a rich selection of Lebanese favorites, from juicy kebabs to an assortment of mezze, each dish more flavorful than the last. The staff's hospitality is commendable, always attentive and quick to provide recommendations. Al Falamanki is not just a place to eat but a cultural experience, ideal for family gatherings or a relaxed evening with friends.
tips: For the best experience, opt for outdoor seating surrounded by beautiful garden surroundings | Visit during late hours as the place stays open till very late offering a laid-back atmosphere | No booking required so you can drop by spontaneously; parking available outside | Try their delicious food options while enjoying WiFi access and watching sports on screens indoors or outdoors

name: Fred Kitchen & Bar
description: About Nestled just off the bustling main street of Gemmayzeh, Fred Kitchen & Bar is a delightful Franco-Italian eatery brought to life by the talented chef Alfred Asseily from Ecole Ritz Escoffier. This charming restaurant and bar exudes warmth with its lamp-lit tables and lush greenery, creating an inviting atmosphere perfect for any occasion. The menu boasts a tantalizing array of Mediterranean dishes, including their signature goats cheese salad and the renowned Croque de Fred.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience amazing food quality and presentation 2 Enjoy a charming and cozy setting with different dining rooms 3 Try delicious dishes like tuna tartare, roquefort endives salad, burger, magret de canard
reviews_summary: Fred Kitchen & Bar in Beirut is a beloved spot for many diners, with patrons praising the amazing food quality and presentation, attentive yet unobtrusive service, charming and cozy setting with different dining rooms, and a diverse menu offering delicious dishes like tuna tartare, roquefort endives salad, burger, magret de canard, pain perdu, and fondant. The atmosphere is enjoyable while the wine list is diverse. Chef Alfred's expertise shines through in creating an excellent Parisian bistro experience that exceeds expectations.
tips: Check for specials on the menu for unique offerings | Visit during Saturday lunch for a quieter ambiance | Don't miss trying the pain perdu or fondant desserts | Consider asking for recommendations from staff like Nadeem or Joe for an enhanced experience

name: Bayt Em Nazih
address_full: VFWP+5GM, Urban Gardens bldg Abdul Baki st right turn off Hamra main street before "Kababji, Beirut, Lebanon
area: Urban Gardens bldg Abdul Baki st right turn off Hamra main street before "Kababji
description: About Bayt Em Nazih is a Lebanese restaurant located in Hamra, offering authentic cuisine and a spacious environment for work meetings or study groups. During the day, it provides a relaxed and trendy atmosphere with beautifully plated dishes at affordable prices. The cafe caters to various dietary needs including vegetarian, gluten-free, Halal, and vegan options. While it's great for intimate dates and group gatherings surrounded by greenery, visitors should be aware that the night ambiance transforms into an old local music scene.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Indulge in traditional Lebanese breakfast 2 Enjoy authentic Lebanese cuisine at reasonable prices 3 Experience a unique dining setting with self-service concept
reviews_summary: Bayt Em Nazih is a charming Lebanese restaurant known for its authentic cuisine, cozy atmosphere, and reasonable prices. Guests appreciate the self-service concept, short waiting times, and the opportunity to play backgammon while enjoying their meal. The food quality is exceptional with dishes like Tabbouleh, Hummus, Falafel, and mixed kebabs receiving high praise. The staff are friendly and welcoming despite the lack of table service. Additionally, live music events on weekends add to the fantastic ambiance of this hidden gem in Beirut.
tips: Visit during daytime for a quieter atmosphere before evening music starts | Try their buffet lunch offering a wide variety of dishes at an affordable price | Don't miss out on their sides like Tabbouleh, Hummus, Falafel which are highly recommended by visitors | Embrace the self-service setup as part of the dining experience; be prepared to order yourself and pay upfront

name: Kampaï
description: About Kampaï is a modern Japanese restaurant located in Downtown Beirut, offering an extensive menu of Japanese cuisine including appetizers, soups, salads, tempura, and various sushi options. The restaurant's Zen decor provides a relaxing ambiance for guests to enjoy their meal at any time of the day. Known for its attention to detail and commitment to quality, Kampaï offers an upscale dining experience with luxurious sushi offerings.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience consistent quality Japanese cuisine 2 Enjoy delicious dishes like crispy spicy salmon and eel sushi 3 Relish in a cozy atmosphere with professional staff
reviews_summary: Kampaï is a Japanese restaurant in Beirut known for its consistent quality and delicious food, although some reviewers find the portions small for the price. The service is described as efficient but not overly friendly, with a mix of opinions on the atmosphere. Customers praise dishes like crispy spicy salmon, ceviche, crispy crab salad, white shrimp sushi, and eel sushi. While some note a decline in portion sizes and service quality over time, others appreciate the cozy atmosphere and excellent staff.
tips: Try ordering recommended dishes like crispy crab salad or white shrimp sushi | Consider visiting during less crowded times to avoid slower service | If dining outdoors, opt for the amazing outdoor area at Kampaï

name: Loris Restaurant
description: About Loris Restaurant in Gemmayze, Lebanon is a culinary gem that offers a delightful dining experience blending traditional Lebanese flavors with modern cooking techniques. The restaurant, located within the elegant Le Gray Beirut, provides stunning city views and an inviting atmosphere. Loris boasts a 7-page menu featuring an extensive array of Lebanese dishes such as hummus, fatouch, batata harra, fetet betenjen, and knefeh.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience traditional Lebanese dishes with arak 2 Enjoy delicious mezze options such as makanek and wara2 enab 3 Appreciate the cozy atmosphere ideal for socializing
reviews_summary: Loris Restaurant offers a mix of reviews, with some praising the delicious food options like makanek and wara2 enab, the cozy vibe perfect for gatherings with family or friends, and the attentive staff members like Ahmad Istanbuli. However, there are contrasting opinions mentioning disappointing experiences with certain dishes like tabbouleh and homos, as well as issues with service quality. Overall, Loris seems to provide a pleasant dining experience for those looking to enjoy Lebanese-Armenian cuisine in a beautiful setting.
tips: Make a reservation in advance to avoid waiting times | Try traditional Lebanese dishes paired with arak for an authentic experience | Ask for recommendations from friendly staff members like Ahmad Istanbuli

name: Tavolina
description: About Nestled in a charming little street, Tavolina is an inviting trattoria that captures the essence of traditional Italian cuisine. With a menu brimming with delectable wood-fired pizzas, rich pastas, and hearty risottos, it’s a haven for food lovers. The ambiance is cozy and laid-back, perfect for enjoying meals either indoors or outdoors.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience exceptional Italian cuisine with rich, authentic flavors 2 Enjoy friendly and attentive service in a calm and inviting ambiance 3 Try varied menu options including traditional dishes, modern twists, and delicious pizzas
reviews_summary: Tavolina is a highly recommended Italian restaurant known for its exceptional food with rich, authentic flavors that transport diners to Italy. The staff are friendly and attentive, ensuring a pleasant dining experience in a calm and inviting ambiance. With varied menu options including traditional dishes and modern twists, delicious pizzas with perfect crusts, and standout dishes like the shiitake mushroom appetizer or pasta, Tavolina offers quality Italian cuisine in a comfortable setting with excellent service.
tips: Book in advance as the restaurant can get busy | Try the shiitake ai ricci di mare appetizer for an unforgettable taste experience | Visit during weekdays for a more relaxed dining atmosphere compared to weekends | Consider trying different types of pizza or pasta for diverse flavor experiences

name: Swiss Butter
description: About Nestled in a charming locale, Swiss Butter is a delightful French cuisine restaurant that exudes a rustic charm with its high ceilings and dim lighting, creating an inviting atmosphere perfect for any dining experience. Known for its mouthwatering steaks and signature sauces, the menu features delectable options like Beef Fillet and their renowned Swiss Butter Burger. Guests can enjoy generous portions beautifully presented alongside house wines and an array of beers. The ambiance is lively yet cozy, making it a popular spot on weekends.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Unique concept of a steakhouse with a twist 2 Delicious meals served with signature sauce 3 Affordable prices compared to portion size
reviews_summary: Swiss Butter in Gemmayze is a new concept steakhouse offering only three main dishes - chicken, meat, or salmon - all served with their signature sauce, green salad, fries (or baked potato), and bread. Reviewers praise the delicious food at affordable prices and commend the friendly staff despite the crowded nature of the restaurant. The cozy atmosphere, smoke-free environment, and quick service are highlighted by visitors. While some mention small protein portions, others rave about the beef fillet cooked to perfection and recommend adding chili to enhance flavors. Desserts like pain perdu receive high praise for their taste. Overall, Swiss Butter stands out for its tasty meals but may have limited options for vegetarians/vegans.
tips: Arrive early as reservations are not accepted; be prepared to wait on busy nights | Consider adding chili to the sauce for enhanced flavors | Try desserts like pain perdu which receive high praise from reviewers | For beef lovers, opt for the beef fillet which is cooked excellently according to requests

name: Meats & Bread Hardcore BBQ
description: About If you're on the hunt for an unforgettable BBQ experience, look no further than Meats & Bread Hardcore BBQ in Gemmayze. This charming little smokehouse is a haven for American cuisine enthusiasts and barbecue lovers alike. The moment you step inside, you'll be greeted with warmth and hospitality that makes you feel like part of the family. The chefs here are true masters of their craft, offering mouthwatering suggestions that will elevate your dining experience to new heights.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience warm welcome and personalized service from the owner 2 Indulge in delicious meats including best wings in town, flavorful burgers like wtf burger and French mistake burger 3 Enjoy perfectly cooked rib eye steak along with innovative side dishes like potato salad
reviews_summary: Meats & Bread Hardcore BBQ is a beloved spot that has made a triumphant return, impressing diners with its warm welcome and friendly staff. The owner's personal touch in recommending dishes like wings, burgers, steak, pork dish, potato salad, and beef cheeks adds to the exceptional dining experience. Reviewers rave about the delicious food including the best wings in town, mouthwatering burgers like wtf burger and French mistake burger, perfectly cooked rib eye steak, and standout sides like potato salad. The ambiance is described as electric yet cozy, making it perfect for any occasion. Meats & Bread stands out not only for its phenomenal food but also for its personalized service and vibrant vibes.
tips: Reserve a table in advance to ensure you get a spot at this popular establishment | Don't miss trying the recommended dishes by the owner such as wings, burgers, steak, pork dish, potato salad | Visit during off-peak hours for a more relaxed dining experience | Engage with the staff who are known to be friendly and entertaining; they can help guide your meat selection

name: The Shogun Lounge
description: About The Shogun Lounge, situated on the 8th floor of AnNahar building in Downtown Beirut, offers a unique Japanese dining experience. Their menu features an array of delectable items such as the Temaki available in small and large sizes, boasting crunchy nori leaf and fresh salmon skin. Another standout dish is the Hamashi, known for its freshness and complementing flavors of thinly sliced lemons.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Enjoy delicious sushi and Japanese food with excellent quality and freshness 2 Experience a unique blend of modern and classic sushi at a trendy Downtown location 3 Appreciate beautiful interior design with nice views from the 8th floor of Nahhar Building
reviews_summary: The Shogun Lounge receives mixed reviews with some customers praising the delicious sushi and Japanese food, highlighting the excellent quality, freshness, and welcoming staff. However, others express disappointment in the slow service, lack of variety on the menu, overpricing for limited portions of maki rolls, and unprofessional atmosphere. While some visitors appreciate the tasty dishes like Miso soup, Mongolian beef, noodles, fried rice, spring rolls, and good service; they also mention concerns about smoking in a poorly ventilated area and slow order processing.
tips: Try ordering from the Asian menu for very tasty dishes like Miso soup or Mongolian beef | Visit during less crowded times to avoid slow service issues mentioned by reviewers | Consider requesting seating away from smoking areas if you are sensitive to smoke

name: Seza - Bistro Arménien
description: About Nestled in the charming back streets of Mar Mikhael, Seza - Bistro Arménien is a delightful gem that offers an authentic taste of Armenian cuisine. This cozy eatery feels like stepping into a warm family home, with Grandma Seza preparing traditional recipes that have been cherished through generations. The inviting atmosphere is enhanced by unique decor and friendly service, making it perfect for a casual dinner or gathering with friends.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience a fusion of Lebanese and Armenian cuisines 2 Enjoy delicious dishes like Sujok, basterma rolls, and Manti 3 Appreciate the cozy ambiance with authentic decorations
reviews_summary: Seza - Bistro Arménien is a charming and cozy restaurant in Mar Mikhael, offering a fusion of Lebanese and Armenian cuisines. Reviewers praise the friendly staff, delicious food like Sujok, basterma rolls, and Manti, as well as the authentic decorations that create a warm and inviting atmosphere. The owner's attention to detail and customer satisfaction stands out, making guests feel at home while enjoying nourishing dishes. Prices are considered fair for the quality of food and experience provided.
tips: Try traditional Armenian dishes such as Mante or eggs with sujuk for an authentic experience | Opt for outdoor seating on the covered terrace during warmer months for a lovely dining atmosphere | Engage with the owner who pays attention to every detail ensuring guest satisfaction | Visit on quieter days like Tuesdays for a more relaxed dining experience

name: The Bros
address_full: AYA tower, Armenia street, Beirut, Lebanon
description: About The Bros in Mar Mikhael is a legendary spot known for its epic burgers that are packed with mouthwatering flavors. The new, larger location promises an even better dining experience. Customers rave about the delicious burgers, flavorful sauces, and tasty buns. The buffalo chicken and bacon beef burgers are particularly exceptional. Additionally, the wings and fries are highly recommended. While the service may be a bit slow during busy times, the high quality of the food makes it worth the wait.
menu_items: Satay,Chicken sandwich,Chicken sandwich,French fries,Chicken Wings,Club sandwich,Hamburger
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Indulge in a variety of delicious burgers such as the Steak Cheese Burger and Mushroom Swiss Burger 2 Enjoy a nostalgic touch with an old arcade machine on-site 3 Experience generous portions at affordable prices
reviews_summary: The Bros in Beirut is a popular spot known for its delicious and varied burgers. Reviewers praise the juicy and flavorful burgers, with highlights including the Steak Cheese Burger and Mushroom Swiss Burger. The atmosphere is described as inviting, with friendly staff adding to the experience. Some reviewers mention large portions at affordable prices, an old arcade machine that adds nostalgia, and tasty chicken strips alongside the main burger menu. However, there are mixed opinions on fries quality and some cleanliness issues like restroom odors.
tips: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds and potential order mix-ups | Consider trying different burger options recommended by staff for standout flavors | Be prepared for large portion sizes when ordering fries | Keep in mind potential cleanliness issues like restroom odors

name: Beit Kanz
description: About Beit Kanz is a charmingly renovated Lebanese home in Beirut that offers not only delicious culinary experiences but also supports underprivileged families across Lebanon. The establishment hosts a Christmas Fair where visitors can purchase exquisite products, contributing to the community. Managed by NGO Beit el Baraka, Beit Kanz is more than just a restaurant; it's a cultural hub featuring a terroir shop, artisanal boutique, and cafe-restaurant. This space provides an immersive journey into Lebanon's heritage and craftsmanship.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience an exclusive oriental ambiance blending elegance and warmth 2 Enjoy delicious Lebanese food rich in flavor and beautifully presented 3 Immerse yourself in a cozy atmosphere reminiscent of a beautiful Lebanese home
reviews_summary: Beit Kanz is a place that evokes a feeling of home and captures the essence of Beirut in its most beautiful form. The ambiance is described as elegant, charming, and full of character with traditional decor that makes it feel like stepping into a Lebanese home. While some reviewers mention smaller portions and higher prices, others praise the exceptional flavors of authentic Lebanese dishes served with care. The staff are highlighted for their kindness and attentiveness, adding to the warm atmosphere. Despite mixed reviews on specific dishes, Beit Kanz stands out as a hidden gem in Beirut known for its unique setting and memorable dining experience.
tips: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid noise from few tables being taken | Try the chicken dish which stands out among other menu items | Don't miss trying the meghli eclair or molasses fondant for dessert | Explore their shop for high-quality zaatar

name: HUSK Beirut
address_full: Al Arz Street, Pasteur, Beirut, Lebanon
description: About If you're on the hunt for an exceptional burger experience in Beirut, look no further than HUSK Beirut. This American-style diner, founded by a trio of comfort-food aficionados—Richard Rostom, Fadi El Hout, and Ramzi El Far—offers a relaxed atmosphere perfect for enjoying delicious meals.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Enjoy delicious burgers made with fresh ingredients and generous portions 2 Experience a cozy ambiance at this American-style diner 3 Try unique menu items like fried dill pickles or creamy mushroom cheese skillet
reviews_summary: HUSK Beirut is a cozy spot known for its delicious burgers with fresh ingredients and generous portions. Reviewers praise the flavorful patties, perfect buns, and tasty dressings that elevate the burger experience. The ambiance is described as simple but inviting, although the small size of the place may limit seating options. Customers appreciate the fast service and attention to detail by friendly staff members. Overall, HUSK Beirut offers a premium burger experience worth trying when in Beirut.
tips: Visit during off-peak hours to secure seating due to limited space (fits about 10 people) | Don't miss out on trying their signature dishes like Southern fried chicken, bacon and onion burger, or hot Lucy | Consider adding fries to your order for crispy perfection

name: MÉTROPOLE .
address_full: Karagulla Bldg, Minet el Hosn, 24 Park, Beirut, Lebanon
description: About MÉTROPOLE is a chic restaurant located in the upscale Downtown district, attracting discerning locals and French expats. The ambiance exudes a traditional Parisian brasserie vibe, offering succulent steaks and generous salads. The menu features a fusion of French, American, and Italian cuisine. Guests praise the fast service and friendly staff while enjoying dishes like steak frites, capelline pasta, and pulet au curry vert.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience exceptional customer service from professional staff 2 Enjoy delicious food offerings including succulent steaks and diverse cuisine options (French, American, Italian) 3 Relax in a calm and cool atmosphere with good music
reviews_summary: MÉTROPOLE is a renowned restaurant in Beirut's Downtown district, known for its exceptional service and delicious food offerings. Reviewers praise the fast and polite waitstaff, complimentary snacks, well-done steak with generous portions of fries, delightful sauces, and affordable prices. The ambiance is highlighted as nice with good music. While some recommend specific dishes like Sphère Chocolat Surprise and pain perdu for desserts, others mention the consistent quality of food but note that desserts may not be the highlight. Despite minor complaints about dessert or slow service at times, MÉTROPOLE stands out for its overall great dining experience.
tips: Try ordering recommended dishes like Sphère Chocolat Surprise or pain perdu for desserts | Consider opting for coffee during your visit | Be prepared for somewhat erratic service at times but enjoy the overall dining experience | Avoid peak hours to ensure faster service

name: COUQLEY
description: About Couqley French Bistro, established in 2010, is a renowned dining spot in Beirut. It has been recognized as one of the top French bistros in Lebanon. The menu offers a variety of delectable dishes, with the steak frites being a standout favorite. Other must-try items include the duck confit and an array of healthy salads.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Indulge in their famous steak frites cooked to perfection with a signature sauce 2 Enjoy a cozy French ambiance ideal for family dinners, dates, or special events 3 Try their flavorful dishes like poulet frites and decadent pain perdu dessert
reviews_summary: Couqley is a charming French restaurant known for its cozy atmosphere and delicious food, particularly the steak frites. Reviewers praise the attentive service, quality of meat, flavorful dishes like poulet frites and pain perdu dessert, and the overall ambiance. Some mention a slight decline in food quality but still recommend it for gatherings with friends or special occasions.
tips: Make reservations in advance as the restaurant tends to be full | Visit post-4 pm on weekdays for a quieter dining experience | Save room for their delicious desserts after enjoying generous portions of main courses

name: KAMI Experience
description: About KAMI Experience is a vibrant urban bar that artfully blends innovative Japanese fusion cuisine with a stylish, street-inspired aesthetic. The chic interior, designed in collaboration with Brazilian graffiti artist Felipe Yung, sets the stage for an unforgettable dining adventure focused on bold flavors. Ideal for sharing, KAMI's menu features delightful small plates like tempura avocado adorned with bonito flakes and salmon tartare served on crispy rice squares.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience the best sushi in Lebanon with a diverse menu offering plenty of options 2 Enjoy unique flavor combinations like dynamite shrimp and bao beef buns 3 Immerse yourself in a chic and sophisticated atmosphere infused with vibrant urban style
reviews_summary: KAMI Experience offers a delightful fusion of Japanese cuisine in a chic and sophisticated setting. Reviewers praise the fresh and flavorful sushi, with standout dishes like dynamite shrimp and bao beef buns. The attentive staff, especially Imad, receive high praise for their exceptional service and hospitality. Guests appreciate the playful presentation of colorful sushi rolls alongside crispy bites and refreshing drinks, creating a perfect mix of chill and classy vibes. Overall, KAMI delivers an exceptional dining experience that is both memorable and impressive.
tips: Try special items on the menu for an elevated dining experience | Ask for Imad for exceptional service to enhance your meal | Visit during weekdays for a more relaxed dining ambiance

name: Indigo on the Roof
address_full: Le Gray Hotel Martyrs Square, Beirut, Lebanon
description: About Indigo on the Roof, perched atop the luxurious Le Gray Hotel in Beirut, is a dining gem that promises an unforgettable experience. This sophisticated restaurant offers two delightful settings: dine indoors with ample sunlight and stunning views or bask in the fresh air on the outdoor terrace while soaking up breathtaking cityscapes. Whether you're indulging in breakfast, enjoying a leisurely Sunday brunch, or savoring dinner under the stars, Indigo on the Roof caters to all occasions.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience consistent 5-star treatment 2 Enjoy calm and beautiful rooftop ambiance 3 Try delicious dishes like homemade chips with labne dip and crispy duck salad
reviews_summary: Indigo on the Roof at Le Gray Hotel in Beirut offers a consistent 5-star treatment with calm and beautiful rooftop ambiance. Reviewers appreciate the versatile menu, attentive service, and delicious dishes like homemade chips with labne dip, salmon tiradito, and crispy duck salad. The setting under the stars creates a cozy and romantic atmosphere for an enjoyable dining experience.
tips: For a more intimate experience, visit during weekdays or early evenings to avoid crowds | Try replacing avocado with artichokes in the salmon tiradito for a unique twist on flavors | Don't miss out on trying the Crispy Duck Salad as it's described as a pleasant surprise by reviewers | Consider ordering cocktails from the bar next door if you prefer them over wine from their list

name: مطعم مزيان - Mezyan
description: About Mezyan, located in the vibrant district of Hamra, is a hidden gem offering a delightful culinary experience. The warm and inviting ambiance sets the stage for an authentic Lebanese dining journey with a modern twist. Tucked away in the Rasamany Building, this magical cafe serves up small plates of Lebanese, Syrian, and Armenian mezze. From traditional classics to innovative fusions, Mezyan's menu caters to all palates.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience authentic Lebanese and Moroccan cuisine 2 Enjoy live music nights with entertaining bands 3 Delight in perfectly cooked dishes like the fish fillet and mixed grill
reviews_summary: Mezyan Restaurant in Beirut offers a delightful dining experience with delicious Lebanese and Moroccan cuisine. Reviewers praise the perfectly cooked fish fillet, crispy fries, and refreshing salads. The restaurant provides excellent value for money with reasonable prices and smooth ordering processes. Guests enjoy live music nights, friendly staff, and a cozy atmosphere. The mixed grill is highly recommended for its well-cooked meat, flavorful hummus, garlic sauce, and pickles.
tips: Visit Mezyan for Friday or Saturday night parties to enjoy the vibrant atmosphere with live music performances. | For a quieter dining experience, consider visiting during off-peak hours or weekdays. | If you prefer less sweetness in your dish, be cautious when trying the Cherry Kebab. | When ordering takeout from Mezyan Restaurant, expect generous portions suitable for sharing or satisfying hearty appetites.

name: Tabliyit Massaad
description: About Tabliyit Massaad, also known as Massaad Express, may seem like a typical fast food joint at first glance, but it offers much more. The chain is known for its vibrant atmosphere and affordable prices. What sets it apart are the exceptional sandwiches served on wooden boards called Tabliye. These sandwiches come with unexpected touches like toasted sesame seeds and generous portions that leave you satisfied.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Indulge in scrumptious normal Lebanese food at affordable prices 2 Try unique fusion twists on traditional Lebanese dishes 3 Enjoy friendly service in a cozy atmosphere
reviews_summary: Tabliyit Massaad is a popular Lebanese restaurant known for its delicious and simple food, particularly praised for their tawook sandwiches. Reviewers highlight the friendly service, quick delivery, cozy atmosphere, and affordable prices. The menu offers a fusion twist on traditional Lebanese cuisine with options like potato, hummus, cauliflower sandwiches, saje burgers, chicken wings with pomegranate sauce, and more. While some dishes like grilled potatoes received mixed reviews, the overall experience is highly recommended by visitors who appreciate the excellent service and tasty food.
tips: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds as it can get busy | Try the tawook sandwich which is highly recommended by many reviewers | Consider ordering the chicken wings with pomegranate sauce for a flavorful starter | If you're vegan, explore options like potato, hummus, or cauliflower sandwiches available on the menu

name: Osaka Sushi Lounge
description: About Osaka Sushi Lounge in Beirut offers a top-notch Japanese dining experience with a focus on carefully crafted dishes made from freshly imported ingredients. The restaurant provides a fine dining atmosphere, professional service, and spacious tables for an enjoyable meal. Guests can expect high-quality seafood and innovative cuisine, such as crispy salmon salad and fresh sashimi pieces. The menu features unique twists on traditional sushi, making it one of the best spots to enjoy exceptional flavors in Beirut.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience delicious high-quality sushi made with fresh ingredients 2 Enjoy an elegant ambiance and top-tier service 3 Try a diverse menu including unique dishes like tom yum soup and flavorful sushi rolls
reviews_summary: Osaka Sushi Lounge in Beirut is highly praised for its delicious high-quality sushi, attentive service, and elegant ambiance. Reviewers highlight the harmonious blend of flavors in dishes like soft shell crab, squid tempura, spicy salmon, and beef teriyaki. The restaurant offers a diverse menu with options like tom yum soup, salmon salad, and flavorful sushi rolls. Guests appreciate the top-tier service and premium dining experience at Osaka Sushi Lounge.
tips: Consider visiting during lunch to enjoy the generous business menu with various tasty options at a reasonable price | If you're a fan of tom yum soup or salmon salad, Osaka Sushi Lounge offers rich and delicious versions worth trying | While slightly expensive, the quality of ingredients justifies the price ensuring food safety and satisfaction | For those not craving sushi specifically, explore other options such as poke bowls or chicken teriyaki for a satisfying meal

name: Salon Beyrouth
description: About Salon Beyrouth, located in the heart of Hamra, is a stylish retro-bar that has been serving as a hub for Beirut's thriving jazz community for almost a decade. The venue offers an extensive selection of over 120 whiskies from around the world and craft classic cocktails with homemade bitters and infusions. Its elegant art-deco-inspired interior and covered alfresco garden area provide the perfect setting for intimate gatherings or lively evenings of live music.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience flawless execution of food with standout dishes like fish and chips, mushroom risotto, prawn risotto, burger, Japanese cheesecake, and pain perdu 2 Enjoy a gorgeously designed ambiance with friendly staff creating a cozy atmosphere perfect for friends or romantic dates 3 Transport yourself to a chill vibe during jazz nights at this chic retro-bar dedicated to whisky and jazz
reviews_summary: Salon Beyrouth is a beloved spot in Lebanon, known for its flawless execution of food with standout dishes like fish and chips, mushroom risotto, prawn risotto, burger, Japanese cheesecake, and pain perdu. The ambiance is described as gorgeously designed with friendly staff creating a cozy atmosphere perfect for friends or romantic dates. Visitors enjoy the adult-oriented setting starting in the afternoon and jazz nights that transport them to a chill vibe. Despite some pricey items on the menu, patrons appreciate the top-notch quality of food and drinks at this peaceful haven in busy Hamra.
tips: Visit Salon Beyrouth during their jazz nights for an unforgettable experience | Try unique cocktails crafted with homemade bitters and infusions that pack an extra punch of flavor | For beer enthusiasts looking for imported options from Germany, England, Lebanon, Belgium among others; Salon Beyrouth offers over 14 types to choose from

name: Albergo
description: About Albergo is a grand all-suite hotel located in the Achrafieh neighborhood of Beirut. Housed in a 1930s mansion, this luxurious hotel is part of the prestigious Relais and Chateaux series. The ochre-colored ancient villa with a large gilded cast iron gate draped in greenery sets the scene for an elegant stay. The hotel's interior combines French glamour with unique Lebanese touches, creating a characterful and romantic ambiance.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience exceptional service from attentive staff members like Mr. Elie Jabbour who elevate your stay 2 Enjoy comfortable beds and delicious food at the rooftop restaurant with stunning city views 3 Indulge in high-quality dishes at Albergo Restaurant amidst warm decor
reviews_summary: Albergo in Beirut is a renowned and elegant hotel known for its beautifully curated spaces, impeccable service, and exceptional staff members like Mr. Elie Jabbour who go above and beyond to ensure guests have a magical experience. Guests praise the comfortable beds, outstanding service, delicious food at the rooftop restaurant, warm ambiance of Albergo Restaurant, and the blend of classic and oriental styles in the beautifully designed rooms. Despite some mixed experiences with room amenities for families with babies, overall, Albergo is described as luxurious, charming, and a must-visit spot for a refined stay in the heart of the city.
tips: Make reservations before dining at either breakfast or dinner restaurants to secure your spot | If traveling with accessibility needs or young children requiring specific amenities like cribs or high chairs, communicate these requirements clearly prior to arrival | Consider visiting during off-peak seasons to avoid crowds while still enjoying top-notch service | For families with infants or toddlers needing specific equipment such as clean high chairs or baby cribs, double-check their cleanliness upon delivery

name: Seray
description: About Nestled in the vibrant heart of Beirut, Seray offers a contemporary twist on traditional Lebanese cuisine that is sure to delight your senses. Each dish is a visual feast, making it hard to decide whether to admire or indulge! The restaurant boasts an inviting atmosphere filled with families who frequent this gem, highlighting its popularity among locals. During my visit for a company Iftar, I was impressed by the high hygiene standards and remarkable service.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Enjoy authentic Lebanese cuisine with delicious mezza, BBQ, and fish dishes 2 Luxurious dining experience with indoor and outdoor seatings 3 Impeccable service from welcoming staff
reviews_summary: Seray is a Lebanese restaurant known for its authentic dishes, friendly staff, and good service. Reviewers praise the delicious food, especially mezza, BBQ, and fish dishes. The indoor and outdoor seating areas provide a luxurious dining experience with fast service. However, some negative reviews mention inconsistencies in quality and poor experiences like uncooked food or food poisoning incidents.
tips: Visit during less busy times to ensure better consistency in food quality | Consider trying popular menu items like kibbeh Naye and Habra for tasty options | Be cautious about ordering certain dishes based on mixed reviews to avoid disappointment

name: Amelia
description: About Amelia Beirut is a high-end and selective restaurant located in Mar Mikhael, offering a delightful cuisine and a unique ambiance. The interior design combines Lebanese traditions with modern style, creating an exquisite atmosphere for gathering with friends. Reservation is highly recommended due to its popularity. The menu features must-try items such as shrimp halloumi rolls, truffle pizza, and shrimp barrel. The staff are known for their professionalism and friendliness, providing 5-star service to the guests.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience authentic Peruvian cuisine in Lebanon 2 Enjoy a warm and inviting ambiance with unique architecture 3 Delight in a menu crafted with fresh ingredients and bold flavors
reviews_summary: Amelia, located in Mar Mikhael, Lebanon, offers an authentic Peruvian dining experience with a warm and inviting ambiance. Guests praise the delightful menu crafted with fresh ingredients and bold flavors, impeccable service from knowledgeable staff, impressive cocktail selection, and unique architecture that fits the street's authenticity. While some reviewers highlight the delicious food like shrimp halloumi rolls and truffle pizza along with friendly staff and amazing music, others criticize it for low quality in various aspects such as food, drinks, music quality, and service. Despite mixed reviews on pricing and music quality needing improvement at times, most visitors find Amelia to be a charming place boasting amazing decor with soaring high ceilings creating a captivating atmosphere where guests can relax and savor both visual delights and mouthwatering cuisine.
tips: Make reservations in advance as the place is popular; reservation is a must! | Try recommended dishes like shrimp halloumi rolls or truffle pizza | Be prepared for potentially higher prices compared to other places | Check if there are any minimum charges or rules before visiting to avoid surprises

name: Ritage Garden
description: About Nestled in the heart of Beirut on Lebanon Street, Ritage Garden is a culinary gem that has been delighting diners with authentic Lebanese cuisine since 2015. This charming restaurant offers an extensive menu filled with starters, mezze, main courses, and desserts crafted from fresh, locally-sourced ingredients. The inviting ambiance makes it an ideal setting for special occasions like proposals or leisurely afternoons spent with loved ones.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience impeccable service and knowledgeable staff in a sophisticated ambiance 2 Enjoy fresh, well-prepared dishes bursting with exquisite flavors 3 Savor culinary masterpieces with balanced textures and refined tastes
reviews_summary: Ritage Garden offers a sophisticated ambiance with impeccable service and knowledgeable staff. The food is described as fresh, well-prepared, and bursting with exquisite flavors. Dishes are culinary masterpieces with a nice balance of textures and refined flavors. The outdoor space is praised for its great atmosphere, while the desserts like chocolate fondant are highlighted for being delicious. Ritage by Maroun Chedid stands out as one of Lebanon's most refined dining experiences, combining world-class international cuisine with unmatched elegance in an iconic setting at Tabet Palace.
tips: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds and enjoy a more intimate dining experience | Try unique dishes that span Italian, Japanese, Lebanese, Armenian cuisines for a diverse culinary experience | Make reservations in advance to secure a table at this popular spot | Explore the cocktail list for perfectly complementing drinks like the bell pepper smash or Moscow mule

name: The Barn
description: About The Barn in Gemmayze is a cozy spot known for its bold and smooth espresso and artisanal open-face toasts featuring a variety of unique toppings like almond zaatar cheese, farmhouse feta with rose petals, and stone-ground almond butter with sun-dried figs. This organic food restaurant offers a menu that includes both vegan and non-vegan options, making it a great choice for those seeking delicious and fresh meals.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Indulge in generous platters with tasty mixes 2 Enjoy healthy breakfast options like chocolate-mango oatmeal and tomato feta bread 3 Experience a cozy atmosphere with abundant greenery
reviews_summary: The Barn in Gemayzeh is a charming and welcoming restaurant known for its generous platters, tasty mixes, and fresh ingredients. Reviewers praise the healthy food options like chocolate-mango oatmeal and tomato feta bread, as well as the cozy atmosphere with abundant greenery. However, there were some concerns raised about cleanliness issues and food quality inconsistency.
tips: Try to avoid ordering steak due to reported food poisoning incidents | Visit during breakfast or brunch hours for the best experience | Consider sitting indoors for a more enjoyable dining experience compared to outdoor seating on the road

name: Internazionale
description: About Internazionale is a stylish gem nestled in the vibrant Mar Mikhael neighborhood, effortlessly blending a chic 1950s American aesthetic with a modern twist. With its dark wood decor and striking photographs of fashion-forward individuals adorning the walls, this bar offers an inviting atmosphere that attracts the trendiest crowd. As you stroll along Alexander Fleming Street, you'll find Internazionale buzzing with well-dressed thirtysomethings enjoying cocktails and conversation outside.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience the vintage vibe and international clientele 2 Enjoy reasonably priced drinks like Aperol spritz and flat whites 3 Interact with friendly staff creating unique and fun experiences
reviews_summary: Internazionale is a charming and vibrant spot in Mar Mikhael, Lebanon, known for its international clientele, vintage vibe, unpretentious atmosphere, great drinks like Aperol spritz and flat whites at reasonable prices. Visitors praise the friendly staff creating unique and enjoyable experiences with over-the-top drinks. The bar offers a suave 1950s American chic ambiance attracting cool crowds of thirtysomethings. It's described as a hidden gem with lovely welcoming staff that takes you to the roots of Lebanese life.
tips: Visit during brunchtime for a relaxed coffee experience or in the evening for vibrant beer vibes | Try asking the barmen for their best shot to enjoy special drink recommendations | Take time to unwind and pause in this cozy spot amidst Mar Mikhael's bustling nightlife scene | Meet Mark, one of the amazing staff members who adds an energetic touch to your visit

name: Malak Al Tawouk- Bliss Street
description: About Malak Al Tawouk is a popular spot in Beirut known for its delicious tawouk sandwiches and surprising variety of burgers. The menu also includes options like Attila the Mongolian grill, Pizzaro the Italian pizzeria, and more. The restaurant offers unique burger names like Juan el Ghoul and Cheezy 3azizi, each with its own distinct flavor profile.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Try their creative named burgers like Juan el Ghoul and Cheezy 3azizi 2 Enjoy unique flavors in their wraps such as chicken and pickles 3 Opt for healthy options like the grilled chicken plate platter with garlic paste
reviews_summary: Malak Al Tawouk on Bliss Street is known for its delicious tawouk sandwiches and burgers with creative names like Juan el Ghoul and Cheezy 3azizi. Reviewers recommend the charcoal grilled taouk plate, unique flavors of chicken and pickles in wraps, and the option to replace coleslaw or fattoush with grilled veggies. While most praise the quick delivery service, some express disappointment over missing orders or cold food at the Bliss branch. Despite some negative experiences, others highlight the fine healthy grilled chicken plate platter with loved garlic paste and refreshing salads.
tips: Ask to replace coleslaw or fattoush with grilled veggies for a different twist | Order using their hotline, delivery apps, or shop's app for convenience | Consider trying out Malak Al Tawouk during less busy hours to avoid delays

name: Das Küche
description: About Nestled within the vibrant Souk el Akel, Das Küche is a delightful fusion of The Sausage House and The Potato Bar, celebrated for its mouthwatering sausages and hearty potato dishes. This culinary gem offers an impressive selection of imported sausages from around the globe, with the traditional German bratwurst stealing the spotlight as a must-try favorite.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Experience exceptional German sausages that are a must-try at Das Küche. 2 Enjoy a variety of food options including burgers, wraps, pulled pork sandwiches alongside tasty sides like fries. 3 Savor delicious dishes in a cozy atmosphere perfect for any occasion.
reviews_summary: Das Küche is an exceptional German restaurant in Beirut, known for its delicious sausages, burgers, wraps, and pulled pork. Reviewers praise the variety of options on the menu and highlight favorites like the wrapped bacon and cheese sausage, traditional German sausage, pulled pork sandwich, schnitzel chicken burger, fries, and 'das beer'. The cozy atmosphere makes it suitable for casual outings or special occasions. Customers appreciate the professional service and recommend trying their desserts like cheesecake.
tips: Try the platter for four to sample a range of typical German specialties at Das Küche. | Keep an eye out for additions to the menu such as sauerkraut or kartoffel salad which may enhance your dining experience. | Pair your meal with Vice draft beer or bone marrow for a complete culinary experience at approximately $25 per person including drinks. | For larger groups, consider ordering multiple items from the menu to taste various offerings like sausages and burgers while enjoying friendly service.

name: Pool D'état
address_full: Hamra Urban Gardens bldg Abdul Baki st, Beirut, Lebanon
area: Hamra Urban Gardens bldg Abdul Baki st
description: About Nestled in the heart of Beirut, Pool D'état is a rooftop oasis that offers a unique experience. By day, it transforms into a beach-like setting where guests can swim, eat, and drink amidst over 20 full-sized trees that create a serene and natural atmosphere around the 15-meter pool. As night falls, it turns into a chic cocktail bar with great vibes.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Relax by the rooftop pool amidst over 20 full-sized trees creating a serene atmosphere 2 Enjoy reasonably priced drinks and bites while listening to good music 3 Experience a unique community vibe where you can meet new people from different backgrounds
reviews_summary: Pool D'état in Hamra is a beloved spot known for its friendly and professional team, clean environment, reasonable prices, and great music. Visitors can enjoy the rooftop pool bar with tasty meals, lively evenings with DJs or live music, and even shoot basketball hoops while in the water. The community atmosphere is unique, attracting diverse people who appreciate the relaxing ambiance and welcoming staff.
tips: Visit during the day to enjoy both swimming at the pool and delicious meals | Check out their evening events animated by DJs or live music for a lively experience | For large groups of friends, this place is perfect due to its spacious setting | Try visiting on weekdays for a more relaxed atmosphere compared to weekends

name: Paul
description: About Paul Bakery, a renowned French bakery chain, has carved out a special place in Lebanon's culinary scene. Known for its delectable array of freshly baked goods, Paul offers an impressive selection of artisanal breads like classic baguettes and unique varieties such as olive and walnut bread. This charming bakery is a must-visit for anyone eager to indulge in traditional baking at its finest.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Indulge in famous croissants and pastries at a renowned French bakery chain 2 Experience a cozy ambiance with indoor seating and an outdoor garden option 3 Enjoy high-quality bread, including artisanal options like olive bread and walnut bread
reviews_summary: Paul is a popular destination in Beirut known for its cozy ambiance, delicious pastries, and quality food. Customers appreciate the off-menu bresaola salad as a hidden gem and enjoy the consistency of their offerings across branches. While some mention slow service at times, the beautiful interior, high bread quality, and premium coffee add to the overall experience. The bakery's croissants are particularly praised for being buttery and flaky. Despite slightly higher prices and smaller portions due to its central location, visitors find Paul worth visiting for a casual meal or quick coffee break.
tips: Visit during breakfast hours to savor freshly baked croissants and pastries | Ask about off-menu items like the bresaola salad for unique dining experiences | Consider parking farther away due to busy city center location but expect excellent professionalism from staff | If you're a coffee lover seeking premium options, be aware that basic coffee is served at Paul

name: ASH Beirut
description: About ASH Beirut is a charming restaurant nestled in the heart of Beirut, offering a contemporary take on traditional Lebanese dishes. The menu features an array of appetizing starters and mouthwatering main courses, all expertly prepared to delight your palate. The ambiance is perfect for a romantic evening or intimate gathering with friends. Guests rave about the flavorful grilled calamari and juicy beef teriyaki, as well as the impeccable presentation of each dish.
why_to_go: Why you should go 1 Indulge in exquisite traditional Lebanese cuisine with a modern twist 2 Experience exceptional meat dishes like duck confit and dry-aged prime ribs 3 Witness Chef Eddy Diab's careful food preparation in an open kitchen setting
reviews_summary: ASH Beirut is a highly recommended restaurant in the heart of Beirut, offering exceptional dining experiences. Reviewers praise the meat dishes, especially the duck and prime ribs, as well as the attention to detail in food presentation. The open kitchen concept allows guests to witness Chef Eddy Diab's meticulous preparation of each dish. The ambiance is cozy and romantic, perfect for a date night or small group gathering. Guests appreciate the warm welcome from staff and the creative touches throughout the restaurant, such as handles shaped like cutlery in the bathrooms.
tips: For a memorable experience, try dishes like grilled calamari, red quinoa salad, risotto with mushrooms, and unique desserts prepared by Chef Eddy | If you prefer attentive service and generous portions of delicious cuisine, ASH Beirut is worth its slightly higher cost | Visit during weekdays for a quieter dining experience or on weekends for livelier atmosphere | Consider making reservations in advance to secure your table at this popular restaurant
